architecture-description: Routing, authentication, API access, i18n, theming, and shared app chrome are already wired.
create-resource-description: Use this example dialog as the starting pattern for create forms.
create-scope-description: Scopes demonstrate app-wide context shared across unrelated features.
dashboard-description: A compact authenticated overview for the selected scope.
delete-resource-description: This action deletes the selected example resource.
example-module-content: The resources feature shows service, schema, query, mutation, table, dialog, and route conventions without real product logic.
example-module-description: A simple CRUD reference for future features.
examples-description: Generic scope and resource modules provide enough structure to copy without becoming product code.
landing-description: A reusable Vite, React, TypeScript, TanStack, Clerk, Tailwind, shadcn/ui, and i18n starter for future frontend projects.
landing-title: Frontend scaffold for structured product apps
name: Name
next-steps-content: Replace the example modules with project-specific features, then update translations, routes, and agent rules together.
next-steps-description: Keep feature work aligned with the scaffold conventions.
no-scope-selected-description: Select or create a scope from the sidebar to view scoped example data.
not-found-description: The page you requested does not exist.
owner-email-description: The email address responsible for this resource.
patterns-description: Forms, tables, query hooks, mutation toasts, and route layouts follow the repository rules.
resource-description: A concise note explaining what this example resource represents.
resource-details-description: A dynamic route example using route params and a detail query.
resource-name-description: Keep names short and easy to scan in tables.
resources-description: A generic CRUD-style module used as a structural reference.
resource-status-description: Status demonstrates enum-like values shared by schema, UI, and services.
scope-name-description: Use a neutral name such as Workspace, Project, or Organization in real apps.
something-went-wrong-please-try-again-la: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
unexpected-error-description: An unexpected error occurred. Reload the page and try again.
update-resource-description: Use this example dialog as the starting pattern for update forms.
